query: I need to create an AGENTS.md file for our new Go microservice that handles payment processing. It has a Makefile, uses gRPC, and deploys via Helm.
This repo doesn't have any agent instructions. Can you audit it and write an AGENTS.md based on what you find?
We have a monorepo with 12 packages and no agent context files. I want AGENTS.md at the root and in each subpackage.
Our AGENTS.md is outdated — we switched from Make to Bazel six months ago and none of the commands work anymore. Help me update it.
I'm setting up agent readiness for our infrastructure repos. They need safety rails, forbidden actions, and credential handling documented for AI agents.
What should go in the agent operating model for this security-critical repo? Agents keep modifying files they shouldn't.
Write agent instructions for this Python ML training pipeline. It has complex environment setup, GPU requirements, and dataset handling.
I already have a CLAUDE.md but want to add a universal AGENTS.md that works for any coding agent. How should they relate?
Can you help set up our repo so AI coding agents can work on it effectively? We need build commands, test instructions, and safety guardrails documented.
The edge-firmware repo doesn't have agent instructions yet. It needs an AGENTS.md with validation commands, cross-repo dependencies, and Yocto recipe conventions.
Write a README.md for our open-source library explaining installation, API usage, and contributing guidelines.
Can you review this pull request and leave comments on the code quality?
Help me write a CONTRIBUTING.md with guidelines for human contributors — branching strategy, PR process, and code review expectations.
I need to create a .cursorrules file with Cursor-specific conventions for our TypeScript project.
Write a Makefile for this Go project with build, test, lint, and docker targets.
Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD for this repo — lint on PR, test on push, deploy on tag.
Create a CLAUDE.md file with project context specifically for Claude Code.
Help me write a security policy document for our infrastructure team.
I need to document our API endpoints — create an OpenAPI spec from the Go handler code.
Can you write a skill for handling database migrations in our Alembic setup?
